data_coverage_summary: The Assets API provides equipment and asset-management coverage, including location asset retrieval, asset creation and updates, asset-type updates, attachment to work orders and work requests, asset-location changes, asset work-order history, asset tags, validation flags, and meter-reading capture.
buyer_guidance: Use this area when ServiceNow needs to synchronize physical equipment context with repair workflows inside ServiceChannel. It is the strongest ServiceChannel surface for OT-adjacent break-fix integrations because it links equipment records to work orders, locations, history, and meter readings.
overview: The Assets section is the most asset-centric part of the ServiceChannel API. The reference shows support for retrieving assets by ID or by location, searching filtered asset sets, traversing location asset trees, working with components and replacement candidates, updating asset records and asset types, attaching assets to work orders or work requests, moving assets between locations, retrieving work-order history, and managing meter readings in single or bulk mode. That makes it a practical integration surface for aligning ServiceNow equipment records and ServiceChannel repair execution around the same physical asset base.

available_data.description: Endpoints for finding assets, loading site inventories, and maintaining core equipment records.
Endpoints that tie equipment records directly to work orders and work requests for repair execution.
data_points: asset setup and replacement during work

available_data.category: Meter readings, tags, and validation controls
available_data.description: Operational asset fields that matter for field updates and equipment traceability.
data_points: meter readings and bulk meter-reading updates

ingest_considerations: This is the closest ServiceChannel surface to CMMS or CMDB-style equipment coordination, so record identity and location matching are critical.
The API appears to support both record synchronization and operational mutation, including asset replacement and meter-reading updates during work execution.
Asset history and attachment endpoints can help ServiceNow present maintenance context without making ServiceChannel the sole system of record for equipment metadata.
No live telemetry feed is exposed here; this is structured asset and maintenance context rather than streaming machine data.

source_evidence.evidence_notes: Based on the Assets section in the ServiceChannel v3 Swagger reference and the general ServiceChannel API guidance covering OAuth, environments, call structure, and sandbox testing.
